prompts.prompt: I'm getting a Terraform error that says the Kubernetes provider can't be configured because the cluster endpoint is unknown. I have both my EKS cluster and my Kubernetes Deployments in the same Terraform module.
prompts.rationale: Directly describes the error that results from mixing cluster and app in one module.

prompts.prompt: I added depends_on = [aws_eks_cluster.example] to all my Kubernetes resources but I'm still getting the provider configuration error. Why isn't depends_on fixing this?
prompts.rationale: The canonical mistaken fix — depends_on doesn't affect provider initialization ordering.

prompts.prompt: How should I structure my Terraform code for a project that provisions an EKS cluster and also deploys applications into that cluster?
prompts.rationale: Architecture question about the right module structure for EKS + apps.

prompts.prompt: Can I create an EKS cluster and deploy a Helm chart to it in a single terraform apply?
prompts.rationale: Single-apply question — the answer is no due to provider evaluation ordering; two applies required.

prompts.prompt: How do I configure the AWS provider to deploy resources to multiple AWS regions?
prompts.rationale: Multi-region provider aliases — a related provider topic but not about the cluster/app ordering problem.

prompts.prompt: What's the difference between deploying with Helm vs kubectl vs Terraform for Kubernetes applications?
prompts.rationale: Tooling comparison for Kubernetes deployment — not about the Terraform provider ordering constraint.

prompts.prompt: How do I set up RBAC in my EKS cluster using Terraform?
prompts.rationale: EKS RBAC configuration — a Kubernetes resource Terraform question but not about the ordering/provider issue unless both cluster and RBAC are in the same module.

prompts.prompt: My EKS cluster already exists. I created it with a separate Terraform config last week. Now I want to add a new Terraform module to deploy my application into the existing cluster. Is there a provider ordering problem here?
prompts.rationale: Existing cluster scenario — the ordering problem only occurs when provisioning and deploying are in the same apply; this case is safe.

prompts.prompt: I have the same problem as the EKS + Kubernetes issue but with GKE — my Google Kubernetes Engine cluster and my Kubernetes workloads are in the same Terraform module and it's failing.
prompts.rationale: GKE variant — same root cause, different cloud provider. The skill's boundary explicitly notes this applies to GKE and AKS too.

prompts.prompt: I use a data source to look up my existing EKS cluster endpoint and pass it to the Kubernetes provider, but I'm also creating new node groups in the same module. Will the provider ordering issue affect me?
prompts.rationale: Hybrid case — data source lookup of existing cluster avoids the bootstrap problem, but creating new cluster infrastructure in the same module may reintroduce it.
